(~a.lifornia 
J1~ir l"olitical 
Jl'-actices Commissioll, 
Guille Libresco, Ph.D. 
683 Bianco Court 
Davis, CA 
95616 
Dear Dr. Libresco: 
July 29, 1988 
Re: 
Your Request For Advice 
Our File No. I-88'-239 
You have requested advice about application of conflict of 
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").!.! 
to your duties on the Yolo County Mental Health Advisory Board. 
Your advice request does not refer to a specific 
governmental decision. 
Therefore, we are treating your 
question as a request for informal assistance pursuant to 
Regulation 18329(c) (3) (D) and (F) (copy enclosed.~ 
QUESTION 
You are an unpaid member of the board of directors of the 
nonprofit Yolo Community Care Continuum (Community Care). 
You 
also are a board member of the Yolo County Mental Health 
Advisory Board (Mental Health Advisory Board). 
Does your 
membership on the board of Community Care require that you 
.!.! 
Government Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
refer~nces are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code 
of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. 
All references to 
regulations are to Title 2, DiVIsion 6 of the California Code 
of Regulations. 
~ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with 
the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. 
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 
18329 (c) (3) .) 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804 .. 0807 • (916)322 .. 5660 


Dr. Gille Libresco, Ph.D. 
July 29, 1988 
Page --2-
disqualify yourself from participating in decisions before the 
Mental Health Advisory Board which concern Community Care? 
CONCLUSION 
Because Community ~are 1e a nonprofit organization that is 
not a source of income to you, your volunteer service on the 
Community Care board does not create an economic interest in 
the organization. 
Therefore, you may participate in decisions 
concerning Community Care, which are before the Mental Health 
Advisory Board. 
FACTS 
You are a member of the Board of Directors of the Mental 
Health Advisory Board. 
You also are an unpaid board member of 
Community Care. 
Community Care is a nonprofit (Short-Doyle) 
contract agency. 
You are not an employee of Community Care nor 
do you have a business interest in the organization. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 87100 prohibits a public ,official from making, 
participating in making, or in any way attempting to influence 
a governmental decision in which an official knows or has 
reason to know he has a financial interest. 
An official has a 
financial interest in a decision that will have a reasonably 
foreseeable material financial effect, different from the 
effect on the general public, on, among others, the following 
economic interests: 
c) 
Any source of income, other than gifts and 
other than loans by a commercial lending institution 
in the regular course of business on terms available 
to the public without regard to official status, 
aggregating two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more 
in value provided to, received by or promised to the 
public official within 12 months prior to the time 
when the decision is made. 
d) 
Any business entity in which the public 
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee, 
employee, or holds any position of management. 
Section 87103(c) and (d). 
community Care is not a source of income to you. 
Your 
services as a director are on a volunteer basis. 
Therefore, section 87103(c) does not apply to your 
situation. 


Dr. Gille Libresco, Ph.D. 
July 29, 1988 
Page -3-
For purposes of the Act, a business entity is an 
organization or enterprise operated for profit. 
(Section 
82005.) 
community Care is a nonprofit organization, not a 
business entity. 
Your membership on the board of 
Community Care does not make you an officer of a business 
entity. 
Therefcra, section 87103(d) does not apply to 
your situation. 
Consequently, you may participate in decisions before 
the Mental Health Advisory Board which concern Community 
Care. 
I hope this letter satisfactorily answers your request 
for advice. 
Please call me at (916) 322-5901 if you have 
any questions about this letter. 
DMG:MA:aa 
Enclosure 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel

By: iMargarita Altamirano 
Counsel, Legal Division 


• 
June 20, 1988 
Legal Division 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P.O. BOK 807 
Sacramento, CA 95804 
To Whom It May Concern: 
/. 
I am a member of the Yolo County Mental Health Advisory Board 
(MHAB), and also a member of the Board of the Yolo Community Care 
Continuum (YCCC), a non-profit (Short-Doyle) contract agency. I 
am not an employee of YCCC, nor do I have any business interest 
in YCCC. 
I would like to know if there are specific current conflict of 
interest regulations governing my participation on the Yolo 
County MHAB . 
you for any assistance you can provide. 
~erelY~L~4J /J1 
GUi~bresco, Ph.D. ,V 
683 Bianco Court 
Davis, CA 95616
